description: Main paper: Two Layers of Galaxy Aging in MaNGA DynPop — A Principle-Level Proof of Concept.
Supplementary appendix: detailed methodology, literature passes, control-variable specifications, limitation analyses, and sensitivity documentation.

Strict-control rerun pipeline: builds the collinearity-free reduced-control model, controlled dynamical-residual young/old quartile split, bootstrap and within-bin age-shuffle nulls, cross-validated age gain, and Spearman rank check.
Primary strict-control summary: young-vs-old controlled-residual scoreboard for the intrinsic, observed, and both-SPS control sets (7/8 bins each).
Controlled-residual scoreboard with bootstrap 95% intervals and shuffle-null distribution statistics for the three strict-control SPS sets.
Per-bin controlled dynamical residual table across the eight stellar-mass bins for each strict-control set; bin 5 (median logM ~ 10.663) is the lone non-decisive bin.
Controlled-model term table: standardized T50 coefficient, robust SE/t/p, model R2, cross-validated delta-R2 from age, and Spearman rho for each SPS control set.
Age (T50) coefficients on the raw dynamical-M/L outcome per 1 SD of T50, with robust SE, p-value, and model R2 for each control set.
Reduced-spec sensitivity grid (metallicity weighting x flattening term x SPS set): all 12 variants give 7/8 bins, confirming the strict-control family is stable at 7/8.
Handoff note documenting the strict-control specification choice, the main 7/8-bin result, age/CV/rank checks, the single non-decisive bin, and the main-text recommendation.
Clean-pipeline v0.6 working draft of the two-layer note (superseded by the final paper; retained as provenance).
v0.6 revision notes: abstract tightening, framework-language changes, and integration of the simulation/assembly-bias pass.
Unified diff from draft v0.5 to v0.6.

Simulation and assembly-bias literature pass: no exact mock-MaNGA/JAM/SPS controlled-residual comparator located; the residual is unbenchmarked against cosmological mocks, not proven anomalous relative to LambdaCDM.
Primary DML sledgehammer report: fixed-mass young/old DML split, 8/8 observed-SPS and 6/8 intrinsic-SPS bins, plus the secondary fDM checks.
DML sledgehammer scoreboard for the observed- and intrinsic-SPS definitions.
Per-bin DML results across the eight stellar-mass bins.
Figure: kitchen-table plot of young and old median DML across the eight mass bins for both SPS definitions.
Figure: overall mass-adjusted young/old DML comparison with bootstrap intervals.
Figure: per-bin young-minus-old DML differences.
M/L split and controlled-residual hardening report: numerator/denominator decomposition and the (kitchen-sink) controlled dynamical residual.
Raw M/L component split scoreboard: JAM dynamical logM/L, intrinsic SPS, and observed SPS log M*/L.
Kitchen-sink controlled dynamical-residual scoreboard (8/8 original specification; retained as supplementary sensitivity).
Per-bin M/L split and controlled-residual table across the eight mass bins.
Figure: M/L component split kitchen-table plot.
Figure: DML component decomposition showing the SPS denominator dominates the raw signal.
Figure: controlled dynamical M/L residuals, young vs old, after SPS/metallicity/structural controls.
Figure: overall mass-adjusted controlled-residual young/old comparison.
SHA256 checksums for the original v0.6 clean-pipeline bundle files, as released.
